data.about-the-iacd#About the IACD:
The International Association for Collective Dynamics (IACD) is a non-profit scientific association. It was founded on 18 June 2026 during the steering committee meeting at the TGF26 conference in Bristol. It is currently being established as a German non-profit association (Verein in Gründung, i. G.); registration as an eingetragener Verein (e.V.) is in progress, with its seat in Düren.
The association gives the international community studying collective dynamics — pedestrian and evacuation dynamics, crowd science, traffic and granular flow, and related fields — a lasting organisational and legal home, and supports the continuity and quality of the conferences it grew from, in particular:
PED — Pedestrian and Evacuation Dynamics TGF — Traffic and Granular Flow
data.documents#Documents:
The statutes are maintained in German and English; the German version is
authoritative (§13). Governing documents — statutes, admission guidelines, and
code of conduct — will be published here.
data.founding-members#Founding members:
The IACD was founded on 18 June 2026 during the steering committee meeting at TGF26 in Bristol, by:
Cécile Appert-Rolland · Nikolai Bode · Thomas Chatagnon · Tao Chen · Mohcine Chraibi · Alessandro Corbetta · Iñaki Echeverría Huarte · Claudio Feliciani · Zhijian Fu · Ángel Garcimartín · Pavel Hrabák · Angelika Kneidl · Gerta Köster · Jian Ma · Alexandre Nicolas · Andreas Schadschneider · Armin Seyfried · Antoine Tordeux · Chuanzhi (Thomas) Xie · Francesco Maria Zanlungo · Jun Zhang · Iker Zuriguel.
(22 founding members, listed alphabetically by surname.)
data.governance#Governance: The IACD has three bodies: the General Assembly (all members), the Executive Board, and the Steering Committee (scientific advisory body). See Board & Auditors and the FAQ for details.
data.purpose#Purpose:
In accordance with its statutes (§2), the IACD promotes science and research and civic and professional education, including support for students and early-career researchers, in the field of collective dynamics. It does so in particular through organising and supporting scientific conferences, promoting international cooperation and good scientific practice, publishing scientific results, and backing young researchers.
The association acts selflessly and pursues exclusively non-profit purposes.

data.executive-board-vorstand-6#Executive Boa:
The Executive Board (§26 BGB) runs the day-to-day business of the association and represents it jointly (two board members together).
Role Name Chair Andreas Schadschneider Deputy Chair Jun Zhang Treasurer Mohcine Chraibi Elected at the founding meeting for a two-year term.
data.financial-auditors-kassenprüfer-12#Finan:
Two financial auditors, who are not members of the Executive Board, review the accounts and the annual financial report and report to the General Assembly.
# Name 1 Iñaki Echeverría Huarte 2 Angelika Kneidl Elected for a two-year term.
data.steering-committee-11#Steering Committee:
The Steering Committee is the scientific advisory body providing strategic guidance for the PED and TGF conferences. Its members are elected or confirmed by the General Assembly.
Members to be listed.

data: The governing documents of the International Association for Collective Dynamics.
data.guidelines#Guidelines: Admission Guidelines — membership categories, admission procedure, and the Steering Committee Code of Conduct These are the current versions. The association was founded in 2026; registration as an eingetragener Verein (e.V.) is in progress.
data.statutes#Statutes: Statutes (English) — for information only Satzung (Deutsch) — authoritative version The statutes are maintained in German and English. In the event of legal interpretation, the German version prevails (§13).

data: Based on the statutes and Admission Guidelines. Some operational details (membership fees, online sign-up) will be confirmed by the Executive Board and the General Assembly.
data.are-there-membership-fees#Are there memb:
Fees are set by the General Assembly in a fee schedule (§4a). The proposed schedule — pending approval by the General Assembly — is:
Category 1 year 2 years 5 years Regular €50 €90 €200 Student & early-career €20 €36 €80 Membership runs from the day you join. Paying 2 or 5 years upfront is discounted (the 5-year rate is four years’ fee). Members may receive a discount on PED/TGF registration — none at PED27, and a discount of up to ~5% from TGF28 onward, agreed per conference. The board may also reduce or waive fees on justified request (§4a). Final amounts will be confirmed by the General Assembly.
data.can-i-promote-iacd-and-recruit-members-o: Yes — every member is an ambassador. You may promote IACD, share announcements, and invite colleagues to join. One distinction: official representation (binding statements or commitments in the association’s name) is legally reserved to the Executive Board (two board members jointly, §6(3)). For official text or commitments, coordinate with the board; informal advocacy is open to everyone.
data.how-do-i-join#How do I join?:
Membership applications are made in writing (including electronically) to the
Executive Board, which decides on admission (§4(3)). Once the website is running,
there will be a short online sign-up form.
data.how-is-the-steering-committee-chosen#How: The General Assembly (all voting members) elects or confirms the Steering Committee — not the board alone (§7(5), §11(2)). Nominations may come from members or from the board; the General Assembly then votes (simple majority). It may include both association members and external experts; board members may also serve (§11(2)). Size: 5–30 members. Term: 2 years, reappointment allowed (§11(3), Admission Guidelines). Composition reflects geographic, disciplinary, and gender diversity; members should generally have taken part in PED or TGF (§11(2)).
data.i-attended-the-founding-meeting-and-sign: No. You are already a founding member — you do not re-apply. Once the membership system is live, you will simply complete a short online profile (and pay dues once a fee schedule is set), so the board has a clean member register. This is a formality for record-keeping, not a second admission.
data.what-bodies-does-iacd-have#What bodies d: General Assembly — all members; highest decision-making body. Executive Board — Chair, Deputy Chair, Treasurer; runs day-to-day business. Steering Committee — scientific advisory body for PED and TGF. Authoritative rules are in the Statutes (German version prevails, §13) and the Admission Guidelines. This FAQ is for information only. Contact and website details will follow.
data.what-does-the-steering-committee-do#What:
It provides strategic and scientific guidance for the PED and TGF conferences —
selection of organizing teams, scientific direction, and the composition of
scientific committees (§11(4)). The Executive Board decides on conference hosting
rights based on its recommendations.
data.what-is-expected-of-me-as-a-member#What: Support the association’s aims, follow the Code of Conduct, and pay dues once set. Holding an office is not required. Members are warmly encouraged to help the community grow — invite colleagues and spread the word.
data.what-types-of-membership-are-there#What:
Membership is for individuals (institutional or company memberships are not offered). The association distinguishes members with and without voting rights. Unless the Executive Board decides otherwise at admission, new members are voting members by default.
Voting members — paying members with full rights. They may:
take part in the General Assembly, vote, stand for election to the Executive Board and other elected bodies, receive all member benefits. Reduced-fee members (students and early-career researchers) — a reduced rate applies (see fees), kept as voting members with the same rights.
Non-voting members — admitted by the Executive Board where participation is mainly for scientific exchange, advisory involvement, or support, without taking part in formal governance. They do not vote or stand for office. The board decides whether they pay a fee.
In short: most members are voting members; a reduced rate may be added later; and non-voting is a special category for advisory/supporting roles.
data.who-can-become-a-member#Who can become a:
Any individual who supports the aims of the association (Statutes §4(1)).
Researchers, practitioners, and students across all regions and disciplines are
welcome — international participation is intended by design.

data: DRAFT — for board discussion only. This page sketches a proposed membership and funding model. It has not been decided by the board or adopted by the General Assembly, and the figures (fees, discount %) may change. Do not treat it as final. Membership is not open yet. The association is currently being established and its registration is in progress. Membership will open once registration is complete and the first General Assembly has adopted the fee schedule. The details below are the planned structure. To be notified when membership opens, write to board [at] collectivedynamics.org. Membership will be open to anyone who supports the aims of the association — researchers, practitioners, and students across all regions and disciplines. International participation is intended by design.
data.benefits-of-membership#Benefits of membe:
Membership is, above all, a way to belong to and support the community. As the association grows, being a member is intended to offer:
Discounts on future conferences. We aim to secure a member discount on PED/TGF registration — none at PED27 (its budget is already fixed), but a discount of up to ~5% from TGF28 onward, agreed with each conference’s organizers and announced per event. Support for colleagues in need. In urgent situations, the association may help members who cannot afford conference or membership fees. This is discretionary and exceptional — the board may reduce or waive fees on justified request (Statutes §4a) — not a guaranteed entitlement. A wider network. Membership connects you with researchers and practitioners across regions and disciplines working on pedestrian and traffic dynamics.
data.fees#Fees:
Draft structure — to be confirmed by the General Assembly once the association is registered.
Category 1 year 2 years 5 years Regular €50 €90 €200 Student & early-career €20 €36 €80 All members are full voting members. Your membership runs from the day you join, not the calendar year. Paying 2 or 5 years upfront is discounted — the 5-year rate is four years' fee, with one year free. Paying upfront also helps build the reserves that let IACD support the conferences directly.
data.how-it-will-work#How it will work:
Membership is not a conference fee — it is a way to support the community, with the benefits above as secondary.
Once membership opens, you will be able to sign up and pay online by card, choosing a 1-, 2-, or 5-year term. Fees are collected once the association is registered and its bank account is set up.
For now, if you’d like to join when membership opens, register your interest by writing to board [at] collectivedynamics.org with your name and affiliation, and we’ll get in touch.
data.membership-types#Membership types: Voting members — the default. Full rights: take part in the General Assembly, vote, stand for election, and receive all member benefits. Students & early-career researchers — a reduced rate (see fees). They are voting members with the same rights. Non-voting members — admitted by the Executive Board for advisory or supporting roles, without taking part in formal governance.
data.questions#Questions?: See the FAQ for details on voting rights, the Steering Committee, and how the association is run — or email info [at] collectivedynamics.org.
data.who-can-join#Who can join: Any individual who supports the association’s aims (Statutes §4). Membership is for individuals — there is no corporate or institutional membership.

data.about-the-series#About the series:
Traffic and Granular Flow brings together researchers studying the dynamics of interacting many-particle systems — vehicular and pedestrian traffic, granular materials, and related collective phenomena — across physics, engineering, mathematics, and the social sciences. TGF28 is organised under the umbrella of the IACD, which provides the conference’s long-term organisational continuity.
More details — programme, registration, and key dates — will follow. To stay informed, watch collective-dynamics.eu or write to info [at] collectivedynamics.org.
data.tgf28--traffic-and-granular-flow#TGF28 —:
The next Traffic and Granular Flow (TGF) conference will be held in Wuppertal, Germany, hosted by the University of Wuppertal.
Conference TGF28 — Traffic and Granular Flow Location Wuppertal, Germany — University of Wuppertal Host Antoine Tordeux Dates To be announced Under the umbrella of International Association for Collective Dynamics (IACD) Dates and the conference website will be announced soon. Details will appear at collective-dynamics.eu/index.php/cod/tgf.
